name.text: Midterm 2 - Q1V1: Magnetic Circuit + Faraday (E-core with Center-Limb Air Gap)

questiontext.text:
Midterm 2 – Q1V1: Magnetic Circuit with Faraday Induction (E-core with Center-Limb Air Gap)

Electromagnetism and Circuit Analysis — LUT University, Finland

Total: 9 points | Penalty per wrong attempt: 10%

Inspired by Ulaby Ch.5 Example 5-6 and Nilsson Ch.6 Problems 6.1–6.5.

Scenario

An E-core electromagnet has a ferromagnetic core shaped like the letter E. Two coils are wound on the outer limbs. A single air gap of length \(\ell_g\) exists in the center limb. The mean magnetic path length through the core (excluding the gap) is \(\ell_c\).

Given Data

  • Primary coil: \(N_1 = {@N1_val@}\) turns
  • Secondary coil: \(N_2 = {@N2_val@}\) turns
  • Core relative permeability: \(\mu_r = {@mur_val@}\)
  • Mean core path length: \(\ell_c = {@lc_val@}\;\text{m}\)
  • Air gap length: \(\ell_g = {@lg_val@}\;\text{m}\)
  • Core cross-sectional area: \(A_c = {@Ac_val@}\;\text{m}^2\)
  • Primary current: \(i_1(t) = I_0 \sin(\omega t)\) with \(I_0 = {@I0_val@}\;\text{A}\) and \(f = {@f_val@}\;\text{Hz}\)
  • Permeability of free space: \(\mu_0 = 4\pi \times 10^{-7}\;\text{H/m}\)

Part (a) — Total Reluctance (2 points)

Calculate the total reluctance \(\mathcal{R}_{\text{total}}\) of the magnetic circuit (core + air gap).

\(\mathcal{R}_{\text{total}} = \) [[input:ans1]] \(\text{A-turns/Wb}\)

[[validation:ans1]]

Enter a number, e.g. 1.23e5 or 123000

Part (b) — Peak Magnetic Flux (2 points)

Given the sinusoidal primary current, calculate the peak magnetic flux \(\Phi_{\max}\) in the core.

\(\Phi_{\max} = \) [[input:ans2]] \(\text{Wb}\)

[[validation:ans2]]

Enter a number, e.g. 4.56e-4 or 0.000456

Part (c) — Peak Induced EMF (1 point)

Using Faraday’s law, calculate the peak induced EMF in the secondary coil (Coil 2).

Recall: \(\text{emf}_2(t) = -N_2 \frac{d\Phi}{dt}\), so \(|\text{EMF}_{2,\text{peak}}| = N_2 \cdot \omega \cdot \Phi_{\max}\).

\(|\text{EMF}_{2,\text{peak}}| = \) [[input:ans3]] \(\text{V}\)

[[validation:ans3]]

Enter a number, e.g. 0.567

Part (d) — Error Identification (1 point)

A student attempted this problem and made two errors. Their work is shown below:

A student wrote the following solution: \[ \mathcal{R}_{\text{total}} = \frac{\ell_c}{\mu_r \mu_0 A_c} \] (Note: the student did not add an air gap term.) Then: \(\Phi_{\max} = \frac{N_1 I_0}{\mathcal{R}_{\text{total}}}\) Finally: \(\text{EMF}_{2,\text{peak}} = N_2 \cdot \Phi_{\max}\) (Note: the student wrote \(N_2 \cdot \Phi_{\max}\) without the \(\omega\) factor.)

Select ALL statements that identify actual errors in the student’s work:

[[input:ans4]]

[[validation:ans4]]

Part (e) — Qualitative Analysis (3 points, manually graded)

Suppose the air gap length is doubled (from \(\ell_g\) to \(2\ell_g\)).

Explain qualitatively what happens to:

  1. The total reluctance \(\mathcal{R}_{\text{total}}\)
  2. The peak flux \(\Phi_{\max}\)
  3. The peak induced EMF \(|\text{EMF}_{2,\text{peak}}|\)

Does the EMF increase or decrease? By approximately what factor? Justify your reasoning.

[[input:ans5]]

[[validation:ans5]]

generalfeedback.text:
Solution for V1 (E-core with Center-Limb Air Gap)

Part (a):

\(\mathcal{R}_{\text{core}} = \frac{\ell_c}{\mu_r \mu_0 A_c} = {@float(R_core)@}\;\text{A-turns/Wb}\)

\(\mathcal{R}_{\text{gap}} = \frac{\ell_g}{\mu_0 A_c} = {@float(R_gap)@}\;\text{A-turns/Wb}\)

\(\mathcal{R}_{\text{total}} = {@R_total_num@}\;\text{A-turns/Wb}\)

Part (b):

\(\Phi_{\max} = \frac{N_1 I_0}{\mathcal{R}_{\text{total}}} = {@Phi_max_num@}\;\text{Wb}\)

Part (c):

\(\omega = 2\pi f = {@float(omega_val)@}\;\text{rad/s}\)

\(|\text{EMF}_{2,\text{peak}}| = N_2 \cdot \omega \cdot \Phi_{\max} = {@emf_peak_num@}\;\text{V}\)

Part (d): The errors in the worked solution are statements {@correct_errors@}.

Part (e): If \(\ell_g\) doubles:

  • \(\mathcal{R}_{\text{gap}}\) roughly doubles (since \(\mathcal{R}_{\text{gap}} \propto \ell_g\))
  • \(\mathcal{R}_{\text{total}}\) increases (but not by 2× because the core reluctance is unchanged)
  • \(\Phi_{\max}\) decreases (inversely proportional to \(\mathcal{R}_{\text{total}}\))
  • \(|\text{EMF}_{2,\text{peak}}|\) decreases by the same factor as \(\Phi_{\max}\)

prtcorrect.text: Correct answer, well done!

prtpartiallycorrect.text: Your answer is partially correct.

prtincorrect.text: Incorrect answer.

truefeedback.text: Correct total reluctance.
You calculated the core reluctance only. You must add the air gap reluctance: \(\mathcal{R}_{\text{gap}} = \frac{\ell_g}{\mu_0 A_c}\).
Correct peak flux.
This flux value ignores the air gap. You must use the total reluctance (core + gap) in \(\Phi_{\max} = \frac{N_1 I_0}{\mathcal{R}_{\text{total}}}\).
Correct peak induced EMF.
You forgot the \(\omega\) factor. The derivative of \(\sin(\omega t)\) is \(\omega \cos(\omega t)\), so \(|\text{EMF}_{2,\text{peak}}| = N_2 \cdot \omega \cdot \Phi_{\max}\).
You used \(N_1\) instead of \(N_2\). The induced EMF in the secondary coil uses \(N_2\): \(|\text{EMF}_{2,\text{peak}}| = N_2 \cdot \omega \cdot \Phi_{\max}\).
Correct! You identified all errors in the worked solution.

falsefeedback.text: Not all errors were correctly identified. Review the worked solution carefully.

name.text: Midterm 2 - Q1V2: Magnetic Circuit + Faraday (C-core with Single Air Gap)

questiontext.text:
Midterm 2 – Q1V2: Magnetic Circuit with Faraday Induction (C-core with Single Air Gap)

Electromagnetism and Circuit Analysis — LUT University, Finland

Total: 9 points | Penalty per wrong attempt: 10%

Inspired by Ulaby Ch.5 Example 5-6 and Nilsson Ch.6 Problems 6.1–6.5.

Scenario

A C-core electromagnet has a U-shaped ferromagnetic core with a single air gap at the top. Coil 1 (primary, \(N_1\) turns) is wound on the left arm and Coil 2 (secondary, \(N_2\) turns) on the right arm.

Given Data

  • Primary coil: \(N_1 = {@N1_val@}\) turns
  • Secondary coil: \(N_2 = {@N2_val@}\) turns
  • Core relative permeability: \(\mu_r = {@mur_val@}\)
  • Mean core path length: \(\ell_c = {@lc_val@}\;\text{m}\)
  • Air gap length: \(\ell_g = {@lg_val@}\;\text{m}\)
  • Core cross-sectional area: \(A_c = {@Ac_val@}\;\text{m}^2\)
  • Primary current: \(i_1(t) = I_0 \sin(\omega t)\) with \(I_0 = {@I0_val@}\;\text{A}\) and \(f = {@f_val@}\;\text{Hz}\)
  • Permeability of free space: \(\mu_0 = 4\pi \times 10^{-7}\;\text{H/m}\)

Part (a) — Total Reluctance (2 points)

Calculate the total reluctance \(\mathcal{R}_{\text{total}}\) of the magnetic circuit (core + air gap).

\(\mathcal{R}_{\text{total}} = \) [[input:ans1]] \(\text{A-turns/Wb}\)

[[validation:ans1]]

Enter a number, e.g. 1.23e5 or 123000

Part (b) — Peak Magnetic Flux (2 points)

Given the sinusoidal primary current, calculate the peak magnetic flux \(\Phi_{\max}\) in the core.

\(\Phi_{\max} = \) [[input:ans2]] \(\text{Wb}\)

[[validation:ans2]]

Enter a number, e.g. 4.56e-4 or 0.000456

Part (c) — Peak Induced EMF (1 point)

Using Faraday’s law, calculate the peak induced EMF in the secondary coil (Coil 2).

Recall: \(\text{emf}_2(t) = -N_2 \frac{d\Phi}{dt}\), so \(|\text{EMF}_{2,\text{peak}}| = N_2 \cdot \omega \cdot \Phi_{\max}\).

\(|\text{EMF}_{2,\text{peak}}| = \) [[input:ans3]] \(\text{V}\)

[[validation:ans3]]

Enter a number, e.g. 0.567

Part (d) — Error Identification (1 point)

A student attempted this problem and made two errors. Their work is shown below:

A student wrote: \(\mathcal{R}_{\text{total}} = \frac{\ell_c}{\mu_r \mu_0 A_c} + \frac{\ell_g}{\mu_0 A_c}\) (correct) \(\Phi_{\max} = \frac{N_1 I_0}{\mathcal{R}_{\text{total}}}\) (correct) \(\text{EMF}_{2,\text{peak}} = -N_1 \cdot \omega \cdot \Phi_{\max}\) (Error: used \(N_1\) instead of \(N_2\), and the sign is wrong by Lenz's law convention.)

Select ALL statements that identify actual errors in the student’s work:

[[input:ans4]]

[[validation:ans4]]

Part (e) — Qualitative Analysis (3 points, manually graded)

Suppose the air gap length is doubled (from \(\ell_g\) to \(2\ell_g\)).

Explain qualitatively what happens to:

  1. The total reluctance \(\mathcal{R}_{\text{total}}\)
  2. The peak flux \(\Phi_{\max}\)
  3. The peak induced EMF \(|\text{EMF}_{2,\text{peak}}|\)

Does the EMF increase or decrease? By approximately what factor? Justify your reasoning.

[[input:ans5]]

[[validation:ans5]]

generalfeedback.text:
Solution for V2 (C-core with Single Air Gap)

Part (a):

\(\mathcal{R}_{\text{core}} = \frac{\ell_c}{\mu_r \mu_0 A_c} = {@float(R_core)@}\;\text{A-turns/Wb}\)

\(\mathcal{R}_{\text{gap}} = \frac{\ell_g}{\mu_0 A_c} = {@float(R_gap)@}\;\text{A-turns/Wb}\)

\(\mathcal{R}_{\text{total}} = {@R_total_num@}\;\text{A-turns/Wb}\)

Part (b):

\(\Phi_{\max} = \frac{N_1 I_0}{\mathcal{R}_{\text{total}}} = {@Phi_max_num@}\;\text{Wb}\)

Part (c):

\(\omega = 2\pi f = {@float(omega_val)@}\;\text{rad/s}\)

\(|\text{EMF}_{2,\text{peak}}| = N_2 \cdot \omega \cdot \Phi_{\max} = {@emf_peak_num@}\;\text{V}\)

Part (d): The errors in the worked solution are statements {@correct_errors@}.

Part (e): If \(\ell_g\) doubles:

  • \(\mathcal{R}_{\text{gap}}\) roughly doubles (since \(\mathcal{R}_{\text{gap}} \propto \ell_g\))
  • \(\mathcal{R}_{\text{total}}\) increases (but not by 2× because the core reluctance is unchanged)
  • \(\Phi_{\max}\) decreases (inversely proportional to \(\mathcal{R}_{\text{total}}\))
  • \(|\text{EMF}_{2,\text{peak}}|\) decreases by the same factor as \(\Phi_{\max}\)

prtcorrect.text: Correct answer, well done!

prtpartiallycorrect.text: Your answer is partially correct.

prtincorrect.text: Incorrect answer.

truefeedback.text: Correct total reluctance.
You calculated the core reluctance only. You must add the air gap reluctance: \(\mathcal{R}_{\text{gap}} = \frac{\ell_g}{\mu_0 A_c}\).
Correct peak flux.
This flux value ignores the air gap. You must use the total reluctance (core + gap) in \(\Phi_{\max} = \frac{N_1 I_0}{\mathcal{R}_{\text{total}}}\).
Correct peak induced EMF.
You forgot the \(\omega\) factor. The derivative of \(\sin(\omega t)\) is \(\omega \cos(\omega t)\), so \(|\text{EMF}_{2,\text{peak}}| = N_2 \cdot \omega \cdot \Phi_{\max}\).
You used \(N_1\) instead of \(N_2\). The induced EMF in the secondary coil uses \(N_2\): \(|\text{EMF}_{2,\text{peak}}| = N_2 \cdot \omega \cdot \Phi_{\max}\).
Correct! You identified all errors in the worked solution.

falsefeedback.text: Not all errors were correctly identified. Review the worked solution carefully.

name.text: Midterm 2 - Q1V3: Magnetic Circuit + Faraday (Toroid with Cut (Air Gap))

questiontext.text:
Midterm 2 – Q1V3: Magnetic Circuit with Faraday Induction (Toroid with Cut (Air Gap))

Electromagnetism and Circuit Analysis — LUT University, Finland

Total: 9 points | Penalty per wrong attempt: 10%

Inspired by Ulaby Ch.5 Example 5-6 and Nilsson Ch.6 Problems 6.1–6.5.

Scenario

A toroidal core made of ferromagnetic material has a narrow cut (air gap) of length \(\ell_g\). Coil 1 (\(N_1\) turns) is wound on one half of the toroid, Coil 2 (\(N_2\) turns) on the other half.

Given Data

  • Primary coil: \(N_1 = {@N1_val@}\) turns
  • Secondary coil: \(N_2 = {@N2_val@}\) turns
  • Core relative permeability: \(\mu_r = {@mur_val@}\)
  • Mean core path length: \(\ell_c = {@lc_val@}\;\text{m}\)
  • Air gap length: \(\ell_g = {@lg_val@}\;\text{m}\)
  • Core cross-sectional area: \(A_c = {@Ac_val@}\;\text{m}^2\)
  • Primary current: \(i_1(t) = I_0 \sin(\omega t)\) with \(I_0 = {@I0_val@}\;\text{A}\) and \(f = {@f_val@}\;\text{Hz}\)
  • Permeability of free space: \(\mu_0 = 4\pi \times 10^{-7}\;\text{H/m}\)

Part (a) — Total Reluctance (2 points)

Calculate the total reluctance \(\mathcal{R}_{\text{total}}\) of the magnetic circuit (core + air gap).

\(\mathcal{R}_{\text{total}} = \) [[input:ans1]] \(\text{A-turns/Wb}\)

[[validation:ans1]]

Enter a number, e.g. 1.23e5 or 123000

Part (b) — Peak Magnetic Flux (2 points)

Given the sinusoidal primary current, calculate the peak magnetic flux \(\Phi_{\max}\) in the core.

\(\Phi_{\max} = \) [[input:ans2]] \(\text{Wb}\)

[[validation:ans2]]

Enter a number, e.g. 4.56e-4 or 0.000456

Part (c) — Peak Induced EMF (1 point)

Using Faraday’s law, calculate the peak induced EMF in the secondary coil (Coil 2).

Recall: \(\text{emf}_2(t) = -N_2 \frac{d\Phi}{dt}\), so \(|\text{EMF}_{2,\text{peak}}| = N_2 \cdot \omega \cdot \Phi_{\max}\).

\(|\text{EMF}_{2,\text{peak}}| = \) [[input:ans3]] \(\text{V}\)

[[validation:ans3]]

Enter a number, e.g. 0.567

Part (d) — Error Identification (1 point)

A student attempted this problem and made two errors. Their work is shown below:

A student wrote: \(\mathcal{R}_{\text{total}} = \frac{\ell_c}{\mu_r \mu_0 A_c}\) (Error: did not include air gap reluctance.) \(\Phi_{\max} = \frac{N_1 I_0}{\mathcal{R}_{\text{total}}}\) \(\text{EMF}_{2,\text{peak}} = N_1 \cdot \omega \cdot \Phi_{\max}\) (Error: used \(N_1\) instead of \(N_2\).)

Select ALL statements that identify actual errors in the student’s work:

[[input:ans4]]

[[validation:ans4]]

Part (e) — Qualitative Analysis (3 points, manually graded)

Suppose the air gap length is doubled (from \(\ell_g\) to \(2\ell_g\)).

Explain qualitatively what happens to:

  1. The total reluctance \(\mathcal{R}_{\text{total}}\)
  2. The peak flux \(\Phi_{\max}\)
  3. The peak induced EMF \(|\text{EMF}_{2,\text{peak}}|\)

Does the EMF increase or decrease? By approximately what factor? Justify your reasoning.

[[input:ans5]]

[[validation:ans5]]

generalfeedback.text:
Solution for V3 (Toroid with Cut (Air Gap))

Part (a):

\(\mathcal{R}_{\text{core}} = \frac{\ell_c}{\mu_r \mu_0 A_c} = {@float(R_core)@}\;\text{A-turns/Wb}\)

\(\mathcal{R}_{\text{gap}} = \frac{\ell_g}{\mu_0 A_c} = {@float(R_gap)@}\;\text{A-turns/Wb}\)

\(\mathcal{R}_{\text{total}} = {@R_total_num@}\;\text{A-turns/Wb}\)

Part (b):

\(\Phi_{\max} = \frac{N_1 I_0}{\mathcal{R}_{\text{total}}} = {@Phi_max_num@}\;\text{Wb}\)

Part (c):

\(\omega = 2\pi f = {@float(omega_val)@}\;\text{rad/s}\)

\(|\text{EMF}_{2,\text{peak}}| = N_2 \cdot \omega \cdot \Phi_{\max} = {@emf_peak_num@}\;\text{V}\)

Part (d): The errors in the worked solution are statements {@correct_errors@}.

Part (e): If \(\ell_g\) doubles:

  • \(\mathcal{R}_{\text{gap}}\) roughly doubles (since \(\mathcal{R}_{\text{gap}} \propto \ell_g\))
  • \(\mathcal{R}_{\text{total}}\) increases (but not by 2× because the core reluctance is unchanged)
  • \(\Phi_{\max}\) decreases (inversely proportional to \(\mathcal{R}_{\text{total}}\))
  • \(|\text{EMF}_{2,\text{peak}}|\) decreases by the same factor as \(\Phi_{\max}\)

prtcorrect.text: Correct answer, well done!

prtpartiallycorrect.text: Your answer is partially correct.

prtincorrect.text: Incorrect answer.

truefeedback.text: Correct total reluctance.
You calculated the core reluctance only. You must add the air gap reluctance: \(\mathcal{R}_{\text{gap}} = \frac{\ell_g}{\mu_0 A_c}\).
Correct peak flux.
This flux value ignores the air gap. You must use the total reluctance (core + gap) in \(\Phi_{\max} = \frac{N_1 I_0}{\mathcal{R}_{\text{total}}}\).
Correct peak induced EMF.
You forgot the \(\omega\) factor. The derivative of \(\sin(\omega t)\) is \(\omega \cos(\omega t)\), so \(|\text{EMF}_{2,\text{peak}}| = N_2 \cdot \omega \cdot \Phi_{\max}\).
You used \(N_1\) instead of \(N_2\). The induced EMF in the secondary coil uses \(N_2\): \(|\text{EMF}_{2,\text{peak}}| = N_2 \cdot \omega \cdot \Phi_{\max}\).
Correct! You identified all errors in the worked solution.

falsefeedback.text: Not all errors were correctly identified. Review the worked solution carefully.

name.text: Midterm 2 - Q1V4: Magnetic Circuit + Faraday (Rectangular Core with Two Series Air Gaps)

questiontext.text:
Midterm 2 – Q1V4: Magnetic Circuit with Faraday Induction (Rectangular Core with Two Series Air Gaps)

Electromagnetism and Circuit Analysis — LUT University, Finland

Total: 9 points | Penalty per wrong attempt: 10%

Inspired by Ulaby Ch.5 Example 5-6 and Nilsson Ch.6 Problems 6.1–6.5.

Scenario

A rectangular ferromagnetic core has two air gaps of equal length \(\ell_{g}/2\) each (total gap length \(\ell_g\)). Coil 1 (\(N_1\) turns) is wound on the top section, Coil 2 (\(N_2\) turns) on the bottom section.

Given Data

  • Primary coil: \(N_1 = {@N1_val@}\) turns
  • Secondary coil: \(N_2 = {@N2_val@}\) turns
  • Core relative permeability: \(\mu_r = {@mur_val@}\)
  • Mean core path length: \(\ell_c = {@lc_val@}\;\text{m}\)
  • Air gap length: \(\ell_g = {@lg_val@}\;\text{m}\)
  • Core cross-sectional area: \(A_c = {@Ac_val@}\;\text{m}^2\)
  • Primary current: \(i_1(t) = I_0 \sin(\omega t)\) with \(I_0 = {@I0_val@}\;\text{A}\) and \(f = {@f_val@}\;\text{Hz}\)
  • Permeability of free space: \(\mu_0 = 4\pi \times 10^{-7}\;\text{H/m}\)

Part (a) — Total Reluctance (2 points)

Calculate the total reluctance \(\mathcal{R}_{\text{total}}\) of the magnetic circuit (core + air gap).

\(\mathcal{R}_{\text{total}} = \) [[input:ans1]] \(\text{A-turns/Wb}\)

[[validation:ans1]]

Enter a number, e.g. 1.23e5 or 123000

Part (b) — Peak Magnetic Flux (2 points)

Given the sinusoidal primary current, calculate the peak magnetic flux \(\Phi_{\max}\) in the core.

\(\Phi_{\max} = \) [[input:ans2]] \(\text{Wb}\)

[[validation:ans2]]

Enter a number, e.g. 4.56e-4 or 0.000456

Part (c) — Peak Induced EMF (1 point)

Using Faraday’s law, calculate the peak induced EMF in the secondary coil (Coil 2).

Recall: \(\text{emf}_2(t) = -N_2 \frac{d\Phi}{dt}\), so \(|\text{EMF}_{2,\text{peak}}| = N_2 \cdot \omega \cdot \Phi_{\max}\).

\(|\text{EMF}_{2,\text{peak}}| = \) [[input:ans3]] \(\text{V}\)

[[validation:ans3]]

Enter a number, e.g. 0.567

Part (d) — Error Identification (1 point)

A student attempted this problem and made two errors. Their work is shown below:

A student wrote: \(\mathcal{R}_{\text{total}} = \frac{\ell_c}{\mu_r \mu_0 A_c} + \frac{\ell_g}{\mu_0 A_c}\) (correct) \(\Phi_{\max} = \frac{N_1 I_0}{\mathcal{R}_{\text{total}}}\) (correct) \(\text{EMF}_{2,\text{peak}} = -N_2 \cdot \Phi_{\max}\) (Error: forgot \(\omega\) from the derivative, and wrong sign.)

Select ALL statements that identify actual errors in the student’s work:

[[input:ans4]]

[[validation:ans4]]

Part (e) — Qualitative Analysis (3 points, manually graded)

Suppose the air gap length is doubled (from \(\ell_g\) to \(2\ell_g\)).

Explain qualitatively what happens to:

  1. The total reluctance \(\mathcal{R}_{\text{total}}\)
  2. The peak flux \(\Phi_{\max}\)
  3. The peak induced EMF \(|\text{EMF}_{2,\text{peak}}|\)

Does the EMF increase or decrease? By approximately what factor? Justify your reasoning.

[[input:ans5]]

[[validation:ans5]]

generalfeedback.text:
Solution for V4 (Rectangular Core with Two Series Air Gaps)

Part (a):

\(\mathcal{R}_{\text{core}} = \frac{\ell_c}{\mu_r \mu_0 A_c} = {@float(R_core)@}\;\text{A-turns/Wb}\)

\(\mathcal{R}_{\text{gap}} = \frac{\ell_g}{\mu_0 A_c} = {@float(R_gap)@}\;\text{A-turns/Wb}\)

\(\mathcal{R}_{\text{total}} = {@R_total_num@}\;\text{A-turns/Wb}\)

Part (b):

\(\Phi_{\max} = \frac{N_1 I_0}{\mathcal{R}_{\text{total}}} = {@Phi_max_num@}\;\text{Wb}\)

Part (c):

\(\omega = 2\pi f = {@float(omega_val)@}\;\text{rad/s}\)

\(|\text{EMF}_{2,\text{peak}}| = N_2 \cdot \omega \cdot \Phi_{\max} = {@emf_peak_num@}\;\text{V}\)

Part (d): The errors in the worked solution are statements {@correct_errors@}.

Part (e): If \(\ell_g\) doubles:

  • \(\mathcal{R}_{\text{gap}}\) roughly doubles (since \(\mathcal{R}_{\text{gap}} \propto \ell_g\))
  • \(\mathcal{R}_{\text{total}}\) increases (but not by 2× because the core reluctance is unchanged)
  • \(\Phi_{\max}\) decreases (inversely proportional to \(\mathcal{R}_{\text{total}}\))
  • \(|\text{EMF}_{2,\text{peak}}|\) decreases by the same factor as \(\Phi_{\max}\)

prtcorrect.text: Correct answer, well done!

prtpartiallycorrect.text: Your answer is partially correct.

prtincorrect.text: Incorrect answer.

truefeedback.text: Correct total reluctance.
You calculated the core reluctance only. You must add the air gap reluctance: \(\mathcal{R}_{\text{gap}} = \frac{\ell_g}{\mu_0 A_c}\).
Correct peak flux.
This flux value ignores the air gap. You must use the total reluctance (core + gap) in \(\Phi_{\max} = \frac{N_1 I_0}{\mathcal{R}_{\text{total}}}\).
Correct peak induced EMF.
You forgot the \(\omega\) factor. The derivative of \(\sin(\omega t)\) is \(\omega \cos(\omega t)\), so \(|\text{EMF}_{2,\text{peak}}| = N_2 \cdot \omega \cdot \Phi_{\max}\).
You used \(N_1\) instead of \(N_2\). The induced EMF in the secondary coil uses \(N_2\): \(|\text{EMF}_{2,\text{peak}}| = N_2 \cdot \omega \cdot \Phi_{\max}\).
Correct! You identified all errors in the worked solution.

falsefeedback.text: Not all errors were correctly identified. Review the worked solution carefully.

name.text: Midterm 2 - Q1: Upload Handwritten Work (Magnetic Circuit + Faraday)

questiontext.text:
Upload Your Handwritten Work for Q1

Upload clear photos or PDF scans of your handwritten work for Question 1 (Magnetic Circuit with Faraday Induction). This is for verification and grading support.

Your uploads must include:

  • Part (a) — Reluctance:
    • The equivalent reluctance circuit you drew from the physical diagram
    • Core reluctance and air gap reluctance calculations with units
    • Total reluctance clearly stated
  • Part (b) — Flux:
    • Application of Ampere’s law / reluctance model
    • Peak flux calculation showing N·I / R_total
  • Part (c) — Induced EMF:
    • Faraday’s law setup showing the time derivative
    • Differentiation step: d(sin ωt)/dt = ω cos(ωt)
    • Final EMF calculation
  • Part (e) — Qualitative analysis:
    • Reasoning about how doubling the gap affects each quantity
    • Proportionality argument

Upload Instructions:

  1. Write your name and student ID on every page.
  2. Use a dark pen (blue or black) on white paper.
  3. Ensure photos are legible (good lighting, in focus).
  4. You may upload up to 2 files. Accepted: PDF, JPG, JPEG, PNG.

Note: This upload is worth 0 points. Your handwritten work supports the grading of the main question. Failure to upload may affect verification of your answers.

Teacher notice: Ensure “Allow attachments” is set to 2 or more in Moodle quiz settings → Files and uploads.

generalfeedback.text: Thank you for uploading your handwritten work for Q1.

graderinfo.text:
Instructor Notes — Q1 Handwritten Work Review

  • Check that the student drew a reluctance circuit from the physical diagram
  • Verify correct formulas for R_core and R_gap
  • Check Faraday’s law differentiation step
  • For Part (e): look for proportionality reasoning, not just “it decreases”
  • Red flags: Final answers with no supporting work, inconsistent handwriting
